topic: Structure-agnostic minimax rate for pointwise conditional-average-treatment-effect estimation: localize the scalar structure-agnostic linear-functional lower-bound theory to CATE at a point x0 under black-box nuisance rates (r_g, r_m), proving a matching cross-fit DR-Learner achiever, i.e. a sqrt(r_g r_m)-type pointwise rate with both achievability and a Le Cam two-point converse.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the sharp structure-agnostic minimax pointwise rate for estimating CATE at x0 when only black-box nuisance rates r_g and r_m are assumed?

source_refs.claim: The paper proves structure-agnostic optimality of doubly robust estimators for ATE, ATT, and weighted ATE variants, leaving pointwise CATE outside its target class.
source_refs.author: Jin and Syrgkanis

source_refs.claim: The paper derives minimax CATE rates in a Holder-smooth model and frames CATE optimality as unresolved outside such structure.
source_refs.author: Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman

source_refs.excerpt: derive the empirical-process upper scale and an actual matching lower-bound construction
replace A-frontier-active with primitive empirical-process maximal-inequality + minimax lower-bound arguments

open_problems.why_unsolved: The web literature splits the problem into two settled regimes: structure-agnostic DR optimality for global treatment-effect functionals, and Holder-smooth minimax CATE theory for pointwise/local heterogeneous effects. The prior CausalSmith DML frontier attempt confirms the same obstacle internally: its proposed frontier had to assume the empirical-process upper scale and active lower-bound alternatives instead of deriving them. A point evaluation of CATE is local and nonregular, so neither a global ATE Le Cam construction nor a smooth CATE oracle-rate proof directly yields the black-box nuisance product frontier.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp minimax theorem for CATE(x0), with a cross-fit local DR-Learner achievability bound and a matching Le Cam two-point lower bound whose rate is stated in terms of r_g, r_m, local effective sample size or Riesz norm, and any localization bias.

writing_exemplar.why: It gives the closest CATE-specific motivation arc: heterogeneous effects matter, many estimators exist, and optimal pointwise rates require a minimax formulation.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: CATE is the estimand for individualized causal effects -> many ML CATE estimators lack sharp optimality guarantees -> Holder-smooth minimax theory resolves one structured regime -> the remaining gap is the structure-agnostic black-box nuisance-rate regime at x0

method_exemplars.technique: Structure-agnostic lower-bound construction calibrated to nuisance-learning rates, paired with doubly robust upper bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target theorem asks for the same black-box minimax logic, but localized to the nonregular CATE point-evaluation functional.

method_exemplars.technique: Pointwise CATE minimax lower and upper-rate analysis using local smoothing and nuisance-rate elbows.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the CATE localization machinery that the structure-agnostic nuisance-rate lower bound must splice with DR product-bias control.

method_exemplars.technique: Cross-fitting plus Neyman-orthogonal score expansion with second-order product-rate remainder control.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The upper bound is a localized version of the standard DML expansion, with the pointwise localization scale made explicit.

open_problems.open_problem: Can one construct a growing report-class lower-bound witness for local CATE DML showing Gaussian or multiplier approximation failure at the claimed entropy-Riesz scale?

source_refs.excerpt: Add a concrete non-corner report-packing witness for Conjecture 1 with a specified growing class H_n
it does not instantiate the non-corner entropy channel promised by the flagship kernel

open_problems.why_unsolved: The prior proposal could exhibit a product-rate channel in fixed or corner cases, but reviewers explicitly flagged the missing non-corner entropy construction. Without a growing H_n packing, the report-class obstruction remains a symbolic assumption rather than a minimax necessity proof, and Stage -1.2 cannot honestly claim a frontier for simultaneous or many-report local CATE inference.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A lower-bound theorem constructing a growing local CATE report class H_n and two-point or packing alternatives whose standardized orthogonal-score process fails Gaussian approximation at the J_n A_n / sqrt(n) entropy-Riesz scale.

writing_exemplar.why: It motivates nonregular local functionals through Riesz norms, which is the language needed to state the report-class obstruction.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: regularized Riesz debiasing handles local functionals -> local/report classes can grow with n -> entropy and Riesz norm jointly control approximation -> a report-packing lower bound is needed for necessity

method_exemplars.technique: High-dimensional Gaussian and multiplier approximation bounds for suprema of empirical processes, including the anti-concentration step.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired witness is exactly a boundary case for when a local CATE score process over H_n can or cannot be Gaussian-approximated.

method_exemplars.technique: Orthogonal signal construction followed by simultaneous inference over CATE summaries.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It provides the closest causal-function inference skeleton, while the open problem supplies the missing growing-class lower-bound obstruction.

open_problems.open_problem: Can automatic or regularized-Riesz DML deliver sharp structure-agnostic guarantees for pointwise CATE when the local Riesz representer must itself be learned?

source_refs.claim: The paper treats regular and nonregular local linear functionals, including effects conditional on a covariate subvector fixed at a point, via regularized Riesz representers.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh

source_refs.claim: The paper estimates Riesz representers automatically from the target functional using arbitrary learners, including neural nets and forests.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Quintas-Martinez, and Syrgkanis

source_refs.claim: The paper localizes DML for target-dependent nuisance functions and proves efficient inference under rate conditions for causal local quantile effects.
source_refs.author: Kallus, Mao, and Uehara

open_problems.why_unsolved: Local CATE at x0 can be written through a localized or regularized Riesz representer, but automatic-Riesz and localized-DML papers mainly provide upper-bound or inference frameworks under representer regularization and rate assumptions. They do not characterize whether learning the local representer preserves the sharp structure-agnostic product-rate lower bound, nor whether representer error creates a third rate channel beyond r_g, r_m, and localization variance.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A characterization theorem giving necessary and sufficient rate conditions on a learned local Riesz representer under which pointwise CATE attains the structure-agnostic minimax rate, plus a converse showing rate failure when those conditions are violated.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames local nonregular functionals as the natural extension of debiased ML beyond root-n global parameters.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: many causal targets are regression functionals -> local targets are nonregular and need Riesz regularization -> automatic/local DML estimates the representer -> the unresolved issue is sharp structure-agnostic optimality when the representer is learned

method_exemplars.technique: Riesz regression that estimates debiasing weights from the target functional rather than requiring closed-form influence functions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Pointwise CATE needs an estimated local debiasing object whose error must be folded into the minimax rate.

method_exemplars.technique: Localized nuisance estimation around target-dependent functions, with cross-fitting and orthogonal estimating equations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is structurally analogous to avoiding global nuisance learning for a local causal target.

open_problems.open_problem: Is there a structure-agnostic uniform confidence-band theory for CATE over x with pointwise black-box nuisance rates and no fixed forest, sparse-linear, or basis-projection model?

source_refs.claim: The paper develops DML estimation and simultaneous inference for best linear predictors and basis approximations of CATE and other causal functions.
source_refs.author: Semenova and Chernozhukov

source_refs.claim: The paper gives asymptotic confidence intervals for heterogeneous treatment effects centered at causal forest estimates under forest-specific conditions.
source_refs.author: Wager and Athey

source_refs.claim: The paper generalizes forest weights to estimate heterogeneous local moment targets with asymptotic theory.
source_refs.author: Athey, Tibshirani, and Wager

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing simultaneous CATE inference either targets a projected or basis approximation to the structural function, or relies on a specific forest/honesty construction. A structure-agnostic band would need to combine local nonregularity, black-box nuisance errors, and empirical-process control over x without assuming that a fixed CATE smoother already supplies stochastic equicontinuity and valid variance estimates.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniform-in-x confidence-band theorem with explicit width depending on local sample size, nuisance rates r_g(x), r_m(x), and index-class complexity, together with a matching lower bound for at least one canonical local-function class.

writing_exemplar.why: It gives the cleanest econometric narrative for moving from CATE-as-function to simultaneous inference using orthogonal signals.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: CATE is a function, not only a scalar -> orthogonal signals permit ML nuisance estimation -> basis/projection methods yield simultaneous inference -> the remaining gap is nonprojected structure-agnostic bands over x

method_exemplars.technique: Orthogonal signal construction followed by growing-series projection and Gaussian multiplier bootstrap for simultaneous inference.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A structure-agnostic CATE band would need the same orthogonalization and bootstrap skeleton, but without reducing the target to a fixed projection.

method_exemplars.technique: Adaptive local weighting with asymptotic normality for heterogeneous moment targets.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Forest weights are a concrete localizer for CATE; the open problem asks whether analogous bands can be proved for black-box localizers.

open_problems.open_problem: Can nuisance-learner and localization-parameter selection for pointwise DR-Learners be made structure-agnostically adaptive without losing the sharp nuisance-product rate?

source_refs.claim: The paper selects among machine-learning nuisance estimators for doubly robust finite-dimensional functionals using a pseudo-risk tied to the influence function.
source_refs.author: Benkeser, Cai, and van der Laan

source_refs.claim: The paper introduces calibrated DML for linear functionals, combining cross-fitting, isotonic calibration, and debiased ML to handle slow or inconsistent nuisances.
source_refs.author: van der Laan, Luedtke, and Carone

source_refs.claim: The paper gives a generic model-free error bound for two-stage doubly robust CATE estimation and studies a local polynomial double-residual regression estimator.
source_refs.author: Kennedy

open_problems.why_unsolved: Selective and calibrated DR methods address nuisance choice for finite-dimensional or global linear functionals, while DR-Learner CATE theory usually treats the local smoother and nuisance learners under high-level conditions. At a point x0, data-dependent choices of nuisance algorithms, calibration, bandwidth, or neighborhood can change both localization bias and mixed nuisance bias, so it is not known whether adaptive selection preserves the structure-agnostic product-rate optimum or pays an unavoidable price.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An adaptive oracle inequality for a selected cross-fit pointwise DR-Learner, with a matching lower bound showing the unavoidable cost, if any, of choosing nuisance learners and localization parameters from data.

writing_exemplar.why: It directly frames the practical problem of choosing among candidate ML nuisance estimators for doubly robust causal functionals.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: doubly robust inference depends on nuisance quality -> practitioners choose among many black-box learners -> pseudo-risk selection targets finite-dimensional DR functionals -> the remaining gap is adaptive selection for local pointwise CATE with sharp rates

method_exemplars.technique: Influence-function-based pseudo-risk for selecting nuisance learners in doubly robust estimation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The same selection criterion could be localized and analyzed for pointwise CATE, where the target rate depends on products of selected nuisance errors.

method_exemplars.technique: Two-stage DR pseudo-outcome regression with a generic model-free error decomposition.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The adaptive theorem would refine this upper-bound decomposition by adding selected learners and a sharp minimax comparison.

literature_map:
Track-B scan focused on causal ML, DML, CATE, semiparametric local functionals, and structure-agnostic lower bounds.
Jin and Syrgkanis (2024, arXiv/COLT) prove structure-agnostic optimality of doubly robust learners for ATE, ATT, and weighted ATE variants, but not pointwise CATE.
Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman (2024, Annals of Statistics) derive Holder-smooth minimax rates for CATE and explicitly frame optimal CATE estimation as the live issue.
Kennedy (2023, EJS/arXiv) gives a generic model-free DR CATE error bound and local polynomial double-residual estimator, an upper-bound template rather than a structure-agnostic converse.
Chernozhukov et al. (2018, Econometrics Journal) established DML with Neyman orthogonal scores and cross-fitting for treatment and structural parameters.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, Econometrics Journal) treat global and local nonregular linear functionals using regularized Riesz representers.
Chernozhukov, Newey, Quintas-Martinez, and Syrgkanis (2021/2022, arXiv/ICML) estimate Riesz representers automatically via regression, including flexible learners.
Kallus, Mao, and Uehara (2024, JMLR) localize DML for target-dependent nuisances and provide a nearby local-causal inference template.
Semenova and Chernozhukov (2021, Econometrics Journal) provide DML and simultaneous inference for BLP and basis approximations of CATE and other causal functions.
Wager and Athey (2018, JASA) and Athey, Tibshirani, and Wager (2019, Annals of Statistics) give forest-based pointwise HTE inference under model-specific honesty/local-weight assumptions.
Benkeser, Cai, and van der Laan (2024, Biometrika) study selective nuisance learning for finite-dimensional doubly robust functionals.
van der Laan, Luedtke, and Carone (2024, arXiv) develop calibrated DML for linear functionals under slow or inconsistent nuisance estimation.
The central tension is that local CATE minimax theory is structured, while structure-agnostic DR optimality is global; the gap is a pointwise black-box nuisance-rate achievability/converse theory.
prior_proposal_map:
Target qid stat_sa_cate_pointwise/v1 currently has only run-state records and no same-qid proposal or reviewer JSON.
Highest-yield adjacent source was _bank/downgraded/eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1, whose proposal targets shrinking local HTE/CATE DML, local Riesz norms, nuisance rates, and a primitive minimax/frontier gap.
The strongest reviewer-prescribed gap came from stage_0.5_to_0_attempt3.json and the bank README: replace the assumed A-frontier-active channel with primitive empirical-process maximal-inequality and minimax lower-bound arguments.
The same downgraded source supplied a narrower report-packing gap in angle4_v2.json: build a growing H_n entropy witness rather than a fixed finite-class example.
The angle4_v3 review also noted a fixed-bandwidth corner for the local-Hte/CATE trichotomy, but that is absorbed into the broader pointwise frontier entry rather than emitted separately.
Failed eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2 supplied an adjacent studentization/variance-report gap for DML/LRO/ADML under nuisance and Riesz instability; it was filtered out as secondary to this pointwise CATE rate substrate.
Study-pipeline gaps visible in the prompt were mainly LATE, Manski bounds, or interval-regression questions and were not promoted because they do not match the stat/CATE/minimax topic anchor.
